compiled_state.title: Implement check_cycle_at_level() in ticket-graph.py
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
Implement check_cycle_at_level(source_id, target_id, level, tracker_dir) in plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py. This function does BFS scoped to tickets whose ticket_type.lower() == level and detects both cycles and self-loops. The function is placed after check_would_create_cycle() in the file (check_would_create_cycle() is NOT modified).

## Implementation Steps
1. After check_would_create_cycle() definition (around line 456), add:

   def check_cycle_at_level(source_id: str, target_id: str, level: str, tracker_dir: str) -> bool:
       """Return True if adding source_idâ†’target_id would create a cycle or self-loop at the given level.

       level: ticket_type to scope the BFS (e.g., "epic", "story", "task") â€” matched case-insensitively.
       Self-loop: if source_id == target_id, returns True immediately without BFS.
       BFS: scoped to tickets whose ticket_type.lower() == level.lower().
       """
       # Self-loop: adding sourceâ†’target where source==target is a trivial cycle
       if source_id == target_id:
           return True

       # BFS: find all tickets at this level reachable from target_id
       # If source_id is reachable from target_id, adding sourceâ†’target creates a cycle
       visited: set[str] = set()
       queue: list[str] = [target_id]
       while queue:
           current = queue.pop(0)
           if current in visited:
               continue
           visited.add(current)
           current_dir = os.path.join(tracker_dir, current)
           if not os.path.isdir(current_dir):
               continue
           try:
               state = _reduce_ticket(current_dir)
           except Exception:
               continue
           if not isinstance(state, dict):
               continue
           # Only traverse tickets at the target level (case-insensitive)
           ticket_type = state.get("ticket_type", "")
           if ticket_type.lower() != level.lower():
               continue
           for dep in state.get("deps", []):
               if dep.get("relation") in ("blocks", "depends_on"):
                   neighbor = dep.get("target_id", "")
                   if neighbor and neighbor not in visited:
                       queue.append(neighbor)
       return source_id in visited

2. Export in the module's public interface if needed (check the existing __all__ or top-level exports at lines 1-50).

## Files
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py (modify â€” add function)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] ruff format --check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff format --check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] check_cycle_at_level function exists in ticket-graph.py
  Verify: grep -q "def check_cycle_at_level" plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py
- [ ] test_check_cycle_at_level_detects_cycle passes (GREEN)
  Verify: python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py::test_check_cycle_at_level_detects_cycle -x
- [ ] check_would_create_cycle function is unchanged (non-regression)
  Verify: git diff HEAD -- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py | grep "^-.*def check_would_create_cycle" | wc -l | awk '{exit ($1 > 0)}'

comments.body: AC amendment (gap analysis): BFS direction â€” check_cycle_at_level(source, target, level, tracker_dir) must BFS starting from target_id and traverse depends_on/blocks edges among tickets where ticket_type.lower() == level.lower(), checking whether source_id is reachable. Starting BFS from source (wrong direction) misses cycles. Add: Verify: python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py::test_check_cycle_at_level_bfs_direction -x (add a test that specifically verifies BFS direction is targetâ†’source, not sourceâ†’target)
comments.author: Test

comments.body: AC amendment (gap analysis): None ticket_type safety â€” when a ticket in the BFS path has ticket_type=None (error-state ticket from _make_error_dict), calling .lower() raises AttributeError. Implementation must guard: ticket_type = state.get('ticket_type') or '' and use ticket_type.lower() only when non-empty. Add criterion: - [ ] BFS does not crash when ticket has ticket_type=None. Verify: python3 -m pytest tests/scripts/test_ticket_graph.py::test_check_cycle_at_level_skips_none_type -x
comments.author: Test
